sentenceStructure.openingStyle: Reflective opener -- frames announcements within a process of thought
sentenceStructure.paragraphPattern: Long-form paragraph-based content, no bullet points in body, structured narrative arcs

signaturePhrases: It's a reflection of where our industry is heading
I'll always be grateful for that foundation
incredible project to be a part of
I also want to sincerely thank
A very special thank you to
I'm proud of what we built

rhetoric.hookPattern: Reflection + Revelation: 'After [internal process], I'm [emotion] to share that [announcement]'
rhetoric.ctaStyle: No direct CTA -- closes with reflective summary and 'Onward' signature

canonicalExamples.text: After a lot of reflection, I'm excited to share that I'm officially transitioning from WPP to MarketerHire.
canonicalExamples.why: Signature reflective opener -- signals considered decision, creates anticipation, invites reader into the journey

canonicalExamples.text:
After a lot of reflection, I'm excited to share that I'm officially transitioning from WPP to MarketerHire.

I'll be stepping into the role of Director of AI Marketing Operations, leading the MH-1 Program, MarketerHire's Full-Stack Human + AI Marketing System.

This move represents more than a title change. It's a reflection of where our industry is heading.

Marketing is no longer just about channels, budgets, or optimization frameworks.

We're entering an era where AI-enabled systems, automation, and intelligence layers are reshaping how teams operate, how decisions are made, and how impact is measured.

The future belongs to organizations that can thoughtfully combine human strategy and creativity with AI-driven execution and scale, and I'm incredibly energized to help build that future through MH-1.

Before turning the page, I want to express deep gratitude to the people at WPP who shaped my career and trusted me along the way.

WPP gave me the opportunity to learn at an incredible pace, build and lead teams, and work on world-class brands, including adidas, Tiffany & Co., Mazda, The Cheesecake Factory, eHarmony, and Pella Windows.

Those experiences pushed me to grow not just as a marketer, but as a leader, and I'll always be grateful for that foundation.

Thank you to Davey Varela, Malia Hanagami, Lauren Iannelli, Jake Meyer, Roger Lis, and Sabrina Malen. Your guidance, collaboration, and belief in my growth made a lasting impact.

A very special thank you to Gillian Collison who gave me my first opportunity to freelance with WPP when I was starting my own freelance agency many years ago.

That opportunity ultimately blossomed into a long and meaningful career at WPP, and truly changed the trajectory of my professional life.

I also want to sincerely thank Chris Toy for the opportunity to build something new on an AI foundation, and for the trust to creatively explore how this industry can grow, evolve, and operate differently when AI is treated as a core capability rather than an afterthought.

The vision behind MH-1 represents a rare chance to rethink how modern marketing organizations are designed, and I'm grateful to be part of shaping that future.

And finally, thank you to Kirk Stanovich, one of the most empathetic leaders I've had in my career.

Kirk helped elevate my understanding of leadership beyond building strong teams, and toward something far more important, showing your team that you genuinely care about them as people.

He was pivotal to my growth as a leader, and I have no doubt he'll continue to thrive and make an incredible impact wherever his career takes him next.

I'm proud of what we built, grateful for the relationships, and excited for what's ahead.

Onward 🚀

TLDR, generated by AI but approved by a human 🤝
Leaving WPP grateful
Leading MH-1
Building the future of marketing with humans and AI together
canonicalExamples.why: Highest engagement post -- full career transition arc with extended gratitude, industry vision framing, and signature closer

canonicalExamples.text: Awesome to see the Reddit x adidas "You Got This" AMA campaign recognized by the Shorty Awards.
canonicalExamples.why: Positive recognition opener -- direct, warm, immediately credits the work rather than self

canonicalExamples.text:
I'm proud of what we built, grateful for the relationships, and excited for what's ahead.

Onward 🚀
canonicalExamples.why: Triple-structure closing reflection with signature 'Onward' closer -- pride, gratitude, forward momentum

openingPatterns.pattern: 'After [internal process], I'm [emotion] to share that [announcement]' -- frames announcements within considered thought process
examples: After a lot of reflection, I'm excited to share that I'm officially transitioning from WPP to MarketerHire.

openingPatterns.pattern: '[Enthusiastic adjective] to see [specific achievement/recognition]' -- direct, warm, credits work over self
examples: Awesome to see the Reddit x adidas "You Got This" AMA campaign recognized by the Shorty Awards.

placementRules: Closing position only -- never mid-sentence, never opening
Thematic tie-in required: rocket for 'Onward', handshake for collaboration
Never decorative -- each emoji must carry meaning
Never: multiple scattered emojis, emoji-heavy celebration strings

structuralPatterns.name: Career Transition Announcement
structure: Significance framing connecting personal to macro
Industry context (2-3 paragraphs) with 'The future belongs to...' vision
Closing reflection (triple structure: proud, grateful, excited)
structuralPatterns.useCase: Major career moves, role changes, company transitions

structuralPatterns.name: Recognition/Celebration Post
structure: Context (1-2 sentences on impact and results)
Team credit ('Huge shoutout to everyone on the team')
structuralPatterns.useCase: Award recognition, campaign wins, team achievements

structuralPatterns.name: Industry Observation Post (Projected)
structuralPatterns.useCase: AI + marketing trends, industry commentary, thought leadership

formattingToAvoid: Excessive emojis -- maximum 2 per post, closing only
Bullet points in post body -- uses paragraph form exclusively
Multiple exclamation points -- one max, used sparingly
Line breaks for drama -- no 'One. Word. Sentences.' technique

hashtagStyle.placement: Never -- zero hashtags is a deliberate stylistic choice

taggingStyle.frequency: Exceptionally generous with @mentions, especially in career transition posts
patterns: Grouped by relationship type: former colleagues/mentors, new leaders, key individuals
Always with specific context: what they did, how it impacted him, emotional descriptor
Recognition posts: 0 specific names, team reference instead
specialPhrases: A very special thank you to [Name] who [story]
I also want to sincerely thank [Name]
[Name] was pivotal to my growth as [role]
